assessments.status_rationale: Today’s news bullets include extensive Iran-war coverage but no CFTC press/litigation release mentioning Polymarket or event contracts tied to U.S.-Iran strikes/escalation.
No bullet references UK FCDO travel-advice changes for UAE/Qatar/Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman/Saudi Arabia, despite regional security incidents being reported.
There is discussion of Hormuz risk and oil-market impacts, but no IEA announcement of a coordinated emergency stock release (≥30 million barrels) in the provided bullets.
Bullets include explanatory coverage of a Strait of Hormuz crisis, but no public announcement by Maersk/MSC/CMA CGM/Hapag-Lloyd of a full 7+ day suspension of Hormuz transits.
No bullet provides ICE Dutch TTF front-month settlement data or indicates a settlement above €60/MWh.
No bullet indicates the U.S. administration submitted a supplemental/reprogramming request (≥$5B) explicitly citing Iran escalation or Middle East force protection/munitions replenishment.
No bullet mentions a SASC hearing with an official title including “Iran” or “Strait of Hormuz” focused on Middle East posture/maritime security.
No bullet references a DoD procurement award to OpenAI (≥$50M) or any official procurement posting evidencing such an award.
No bullet references Dangote Refinery exporting finished gasoline to a non-Nigerian destination, nor any corroborating government/shipping-tracking confirmation.
